evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.kt. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes and show a rectangle highlight on each one.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest SDK version, writes complete BarcodeAr integration code into EmptyActivity.kt (BarcodeAr constructor, BarcodeArListener, BarcodeArHighlightProvider with rectangle highlights, BarcodeArView lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding com.scandit.datacapture:barcode and com.scandit.datacapture:core Gradle dependencies
Setup checklist mentions adding CAMERA permission to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting CAMERA permission at runtime
A license key placeholder string is present
No symbologies other than EAN13_UPCA and QR are enabled
highlightForBarcode is overridden and returns a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight
callback.onData( is used to deliver the highlight
barcodeArView.start() is called after providers are assigned
No Camera object is constructed and no setFrameSource is called

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR Activity. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArAnnotationProvider to the Activity, implements annotationForBarcode to return a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation with the barcode data as a body row, and assigns it to barcodeArView.annotationProvider.

assertions.text: BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is constructed for each barcode
The barcode data (barcode.data) is used as annotation body content
callback.onData( is used to deliver the annotation
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, listener, highlight provider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR Android app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeAr.feedback to a BarcodeArFeedback with no sound or vibration (empty constructor or equivalent).

assertions.text: barcodeAr.feedback is reassigned to suppress audio and haptic feedback (BarcodeArFeedback() empty constructor or equivalent)
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, listener, highlight provider) is preserved
